Returns win rate, total PnL, max drawdown, and Sharpe ratio. Use to evaluate a strategy before running it live.\",\n    category: \"data-bearing\",\n    parameters: {\n      type: \"object\",\n      properties: {\n        strategy: {\n          type: \"string\",\n          description: 'Strategy type: \"buy_and_hold\", \"mean_reversion\", or \"momentum\"',\n          enum: [\"buy_and_hold\", \"mean_reversion\", \"momentum\"],\n        },\n        from_asset: {\n          type: \"string\",\n          description: 'Asset pair — from asset (e.g. \"TON\")',\n        },\n        to_asset: {\n          type: \"string\",\n          description: \"Asset pair — to asset (jetton address)\",\n        },\n        entry_threshold_percent: {\n          type: \"number\",\n          description: \"For mean_reversion/momentum: price change % that triggers a buy (default 2)\",\n          minimum: 0,\n        },\n        exit_threshold_percent: {\n          type: \"number\",\n          description: \"Profit % at which the strategy exits (default 5)\",\n          minimum: 0,\n        },\n        stop_loss_percent: {\n          type: \"number\",\n          description: \"Loss % that triggers a stop-loss exit (default 5)\",\n          minimum: 0,\n        },\n        lookback_days: {\n          type: \"integer\",\n          description: \"Number of days of journal history to use (default 30, max 365)\",\n          minimum: 1,\n          maximum: 365,\n        },\n      },\n      required: [\"strategy\", \"from_asset\", \"to_asset\"],\n    },\n    execute: async (params, _context) => {\n      const {\n        strategy,\n        from_asset,\n        to_asset,\n        entry_threshold_percent = 2,\n        exit_threshold_percent = 5,\n        stop_loss_percent = 5,\n        lookback_days = 30,\n      } = params;\n      try {\n        const since = Date.now() - lookback_days * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000;\n\n        const trades = sdk.db\n          .prepare(\n            `SELECT * FROM trade_journal\n             WHERE from_asset = ? AND to_asset = ? AND timestamp >= ? AND status = 'closed'\n             ORDER BY timestamp ASC`\n          )\n          .all(from_asset, to_asset, since);\n\n        if (trades.length < 2) {\n          return {\n            success: true,\n            data: {\n              strategy,\n              from_asset,\n              to_asset,\n              note: `Not enough closed trades (${trades.length}) to backtest. Need at least 2.`,\n              simulated_trades: 0,\n            },\n          };\n        }\n\n        let capital = 1000; // virtual starting capital\n        const initialCapital = capital;\n        let wins = 0;\n        let losses = 0;\n        let maxCapital = capital;\n        let minCapital = capital;\n        const returns = [];\n\n        for (let i = 1; i < trades.length; i++) {\n          const prev = trades[i - 1];\n          const curr = trades[i];\n\n          const prevPnlPct = prev.pnl_percent ?? 0;\n          let shouldBuy = false;\n\n          if (strategy === \"buy_and_hold\") {\n            shouldBuy = true;\n          } else if (strategy === \"mean_reversion\") {\n            shouldBuy = prevPnlPct <= -entry_threshold_percent;\n          } else if (strategy === \"momentum\") {\n            shouldBuy = prevPnlPct >= entry_threshold_percent;\n          }\n\n          if (!shouldBuy) continue;\n\n          const tradeReturn = curr.pnl_percent ?? 0;\n          const exitedAtProfit = tradeReturn >= exit_threshold_percent;\n          const exitedAtStop = tradeReturn <= -stop_loss_percent;\n          const effectiveReturn = exitedAtStop ? -stop_loss_percent : (exitedAtProfit ? exit_threshold_percent : tradeReturn);\n\n          const prevCapital = capital;\n          capital = capital * (1 + effectiveReturn / 100);\n          const ret = (capital - prevCapital) / prevCapital;\n          returns.push(ret);\n\n          if (capital > maxCapital) maxCapital = capital;\n          if (capital < minCapital) minCapital = capital;\n\n          if (effectiveReturn > 0) wins += 1;\n          else losses += 1;\n        }\n\n        const totalTrades = wins + losses;\n        const totalPnl = capital - initialCapital;\n        const totalPnlPercent = (totalPnl / initialCapital) * 100;\n        const winRate = totalTrades > 0 ? wins / totalTrades : 0;\n        const maxDrawdown = maxCapital > 0 ? ((maxCapital - minCapital) / maxCapital) * 100 : 0;\n\n        // Sharpe ratio (simplified, assuming risk-free rate = 0)\n        let sharpe = null;\n        if (returns.length > 1) {\n          const mean = returns.reduce((s, r) => s + r, 0) / returns.length;\n          const variance = returns.reduce((s, r) => s + (r - mean) ** 2, 0) / returns.length;\n          const stddev = Math.sqrt(variance);\n          sharpe = stddev > 0 ? parseFloat((mean / stddev).toFixed(4)) : null;\n        }\n\n        return {\n          success: true,\n          data: {\n            strategy,\n            from_asset,\n            to_asset,\n            lookback_days,\n            journal_trades_analysed: trades.length,\n            simulated_trades: totalTrades,\n            wins,\n            losses,\n            win_rate: parseFloat(winRate.toFixed(4)),\n            total_pnl: parseFloat(totalPnl.toFixed(4)),\n            total_pnl_percent: parseFloat(totalPnlPercent.toFixed(2)),\n            max_drawdown_percent: parseFloat(maxDrawdown.toFixed(2)),\n            sharpe_ratio: sharpe,\n            final_capital: parseFloat(capital.toFixed(4)),\n          },\n        };\n      } catch (err) {\n        sdk.log.error(`ton_trading_backtest failed: ${err.message}`);\n        return { success: false, error: String(err.message).slice(0, 500) };\n      }\n    },\n  },\n\n  // ── P2 Tools: Risk Management ──────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n  // ── Tool 17: ton_trading_calculate_risk_metrics ────────────────────────────\n  {\n    name: \"ton_trading_calculate_risk_metrics\",\n    description:\n      \"Calculate risk metrics from the trade journal: Value at Risk (VaR), maximum drawdown, Sharpe ratio, and win/loss statistics. Returns a risk summary to guide position sizing.\",\n    category: \"data-bearing\",\n    parameters: {\n      type: \"object\",\n      properties: {\n        mode: {\n          type: \"string\",\n          description: 'Analyse \"real\" trades, \"simulation\" trades, or \"all\" (default \"all\")',\n          enum: [\"real\", \"simulation\", \"all\"],\n        },\n        lookback_days: {\n          type: \"integer\",\n          description: \"Number of days of history to include (default 30)\",\n          minimum: 1,\n          maximum: 365,\n        },\n        confidence_level: {\n          type: \"number\",\n          description: \"VaR confidence level (0.9–0.99, default 0.95)\",\n          minimum: 0.9,\n          maximum: 0.99,\n        },\n      },\n    },\n    execute: async (params, _context) => {\n      const { mode = \"all\", lookback_days = 30, confidence_level = 0.95 } = params;\n      try {\n        const since = Date.now() - lookback_days * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000;\n\n        const [modeClause, modeParams] = mode === \"all\"\n          ? [\"\", []]\n          : [\"AND mode = ?\", [mode]];\n        const trades = sdk.db\n          .prepare(\n            `SELECT pnl_percent FROM trade_journal\n             WHERE status = 'closed' AND timestamp >= ? ${modeClause}\n             ORDER BY timestamp ASC`\n          )\n          .all(since, ...modeParams);\n\n        if (trades.length === 0) {\n          return {\n            success: true,\n            data: { mode, lookback_days, note: \"No closed trades found in this period\", trades_analysed: 0 },\n          };\n        }\n\n        const returns = trades.map((t) => (t.pnl_percent ?? 0) / 100);\n\n        const sorted = [...returns].sort((a, b) => a - b);\n        const varIndex = Math.floor((1 - confidence_level) * sorted.length);\n        const var95 = sorted[varIndex] ?? sorted[0];\n\n        const mean = returns.reduce((s, r) => s + r, 0) / returns.length;\n        const variance = returns.reduce((s, r) => s + (r - mean) ** 2, 0) / returns.length;\n        const stddev = Math.sqrt(variance);\n        const sharpe = stddev > 0 ? parseFloat((mean / stddev).toFixed(4)) : null;\n\n        // Max drawdown\n        let peak = 1;\n        let trough = 1;\n        let capital = 1;\n        let maxDrawdown = 0;\n        for (const r of returns) {\n          capital *= 1 + r;\n          if (capital > peak) { peak = capital; trough = capital; }\n          if (capital < trough) {\n            trough = capital;\n            const dd = (peak - trough) / peak;\n            if (dd > maxDrawdown) maxDrawdown = dd;\n          }\n        }\n\n        const wins = returns.filter((r) => r > 0).length;\n        const losses = returns.filter((r) => r < 0).length;\n        const avgWin = wins > 0 ? returns.filter((r) => r > 0).reduce((s, r) => s + r, 0) / wins : 0;\n        const avgLoss = losses > 0 ? Math.abs(returns.filter((r) => r < 0).reduce((s, r) => s + r, 0) / losses) : 0;\n        const profitFactor = avgLoss > 0 ? parseFloat((avgWin / avgLoss).toFixed(4)) : null;\n\n        return {\n          success: true,\n          data: {\n            mode,\n            lookback_days,\n            trades_analysed: trades.length,\n            win_rate: parseFloat((wins / trades.length).toFixed(4)),\n            avg_win_percent: parseFloat((avgWin * 100).toFixed(2)),\n            avg_loss_percent: parseFloat((avgLoss * 100).toFixed(2)),\n            profit_factor: profitFactor,\n            sharpe_ratio: sharpe,\n            max_drawdown_percent: parseFloat((maxDrawdown * 100).toFixed(2)),\n            value_at_risk_percent: parseFloat((Math.abs(var95) * 100).toFixed(2)),\n            confidence_level,\n          },\n        };\n      } catch (err) {\n        sdk.log.error(`ton_trading_calculate_risk_metrics failed: ${err.message}`);\n        return { success: false, error: String(err.message).slice(0, 500) };\n      }\n    },\n  },\n\n  // ── Tool 18: ton_trading_set_stop_loss ─────────────────────────────────────\n  {\n    name: \"ton_trading_set_stop_loss\",\n    description:\n      \"Register a stop-loss (and optional take-profit) rule for an open trade in the journal. The LLM should check this rule on each market data update and close the trade if triggered.\",\n    category: \"action\",\n    parameters: {\n      type: \"object\",\n      properties: {\n        trade_id: {\n          type: \"integer\",\n          description: \"Journal trade ID to protect\",\n        },\n        entry_price: {\n          type: \"number\",\n          description: \"Price at which the position was opened (in quote asset units)\",\n        },\n        stop_loss_percent: {\n          type: \"number\",\n          description: \"Percentage loss that triggers the stop-loss (e.g. 5 = close at -5%)\",\n          minimum: 0.1,\n          maximum: 99,\n        },\n        take_profit_percent: {\n          type: \"number\",\n          description: \"Optional profit percentage that triggers the take-profit exit\",\n          minimum: 0.1,\n        },\n      },\n      required: [\"trade_id\", \"entry_price\", \"stop_loss_percent\"],\n    },\n    execute: async (params, _context) => {\n      const { trade_id, entry_price, stop_loss_percent, take_profit_percent } = params;\n      try {\n        const entry = sdk.db\n          .prepare(\"SELECT id, status FROM trade_journal WHERE id = ?\")\n          .get(trade_id);\n\n        if (!entry) {\n          return { success: false, error: `Trade ${trade_id} not found` };\n        }\n        if (entry.status === \"closed\") {\n          return { success: false, error: `Trade ${trade_id} is already closed` };\n        }\n\n        const ruleId = sdk.db\n          .prepare(\n            `INSERT INTO stop_loss_rules (trade_id, stop_loss_percent, take_profit_percent, entry_price, created_at)\n             VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`\n          )\n          .run(trade_id, stop_loss_percent, take_profit_percent ?? null, entry_price, Date.now())\n          .lastInsertRowid;\n\n        const stopLossPrice = entry_price * (1 - stop_loss_percent / 100);\n        const takeProfitPrice = take_profit_percent != null\n          ? entry_price * (1 + take_profit_percent / 100)\n          : null;\n\n        sdk.log.info(`Stop-loss rule #${ruleId} set for trade #${trade_id}: SL=${stopLossPrice.toFixed(4)}`);\n\n        return {\n          success: true,\n          data: {\n            rule_id: ruleId,\n            trade_id,\n            entry_price,\n            stop_loss_price: parseFloat(stopLossPrice.toFixed(6)),\n            take_profit_price: takeProfitPrice != null ? parseFloat(takeProfitPrice.toFixed(6)) : null,\n            stop_loss_percent,\n            take_profit_percent: take_profit_percent ?? null,\n            status: \"active\",\n          },\n        };\n      } catch (err) {\n        sdk.log.error(`ton_trading_set_stop_loss failed: ${err.message}`);\n        return { success: false, error: String(err.message).slice(0, 500) };\n      }\n    },\n  },\n\n  // ── Tool 19: ton_trading_check_stop_loss ───────────────────────────────────\n  {\n    name: \"ton_trading_check_stop_loss\",\n    description:\n      \"Query active stop-loss and take-profit rules for open trades and check whether the current market price has triggered any of them. Returns triggered rules with a recommended action. Call this after every ton_trading_get_market_data to enforce risk limits.\",\n    category: \"data-bearing\",\n    parameters: {\n      type: \"object\",\n      properties: {\n        current_price: {\n          type: \"number\",\n          description: \"Current market price of the asset (in quote asset units)\",\n        },\n        trade_id: {\n          type: \"integer\",\n          description: \"Optional: check rules only for this specific trade ID. If omitted, all active rules are checked.\",\n        },\n      },\n      required: [\"current_price\"],\n    },\n    execute: async (params, _context) => {\n      const { current_price, trade_id } = params;\n      try {\n        const query = trade_id != null\n          ? \"SELECT * FROM stop_loss_rules WHERE status = 'active' AND trade_id = ?\"\n          : \"SELECT * FROM stop_loss_rules WHERE status = 'active'\";\n        const args = trade_id != null ? [trade_id] : [];\n\n        const activeRules = sdk.db.prepare(query).all(...args);\n\n        const triggered = [];\n        const safe = [];\n\n        for (const rule of activeRules) {\n          const stopLossPrice = rule.entry_price * (1 - rule.stop_loss_percent / 100);\n          const takeProfitPrice = rule.take_profit_percent != null\n            ? rule.entry_price * (1 + rule.take_profit_percent / 100)\n            : null;\n\n          const stopLossHit = current_price <= stopLossPrice;\n          const takeProfitHit = takeProfitPrice != null && current_price >= takeProfitPrice;\n\n          const annotated = {\n            rule_id: rule.id,\n            trade_id: rule.trade_id,\n            entry_price: rule.entry_price,\n            current_price,\n            stop_loss_price: parseFloat(stopLossPrice.toFixed(6)),\n            take_profit_price: takeProfitPrice != null ? parseFloat(takeProfitPrice.toFixed(6)) : null,\n            stop_loss_percent: rule.stop_loss_percent,\n            take_profit_percent: rule.take_profit_percent ?? null,\n            stop_loss_hit: stopLossHit,\n            take_profit_hit: takeProfitHit,\n          };\n\n          if (stopLossHit || takeProfitHit) {\n            annotated.action = stopLossHit ? \"stop_loss\" : \"take_profit\";\n            triggered.push(annotated);\n          } else {\n            safe.push(annotated);\n          }\n        }\n\n        if (triggered.length > 0) {\n          sdk.log.info(`ton_trading_check_stop_loss: ${triggered.length} rule(s) triggered at price ${current_price}`);\n        }\n\n        return {\n          success: true,\n          data: {\n            current_price,\n            active_rules: activeRules.length,\n            triggered_rules: triggered,\n            safe_rules: safe,\n            note: triggered.length > 0\n              ? `${triggered.length} rule(s) triggered — close affected trades using ton_trading_record_trade, then update rule status to 'triggered'`\n              : \"No rules triggered — all positions within limits\",\n          },\n        };\n      } catch (err) {\n        sdk.log.error(`ton_trading_check_stop_loss failed: ${err.message}`);\n        return { success: false, error: String(err.message).slice(0, 500) };\n      }\n    },\n  },\n\n  // ── Tool 21: ton_trading_get_optimal_position_size ─────────────────────────\n  {\n    name: \"ton_trading_get_optimal_position_size\",\n    description:\n      \"Calculate the optimal position size for a trade using the Kelly Criterion and fixed-fraction methods, based on historical win rate and risk/reward ratio from the trade journal.\",\n    category: \"data-bearing\",\n    parameters: {\n      type: \"object\",\n      properties: {\n        mode: {\n          type: \"string\",\n          description: 'Use \"real\" or \"simulation\" trade history (default \"simulation\")',\n          enum: [\"real\", \"simulation\"],\n        },\n        risk_percent: {\n          type: \"number\",\n          description: \"Maximum percentage of balance to risk per trade for fixed-fraction method (default 2)\",\n          minimum: 0.1,\n          maximum: 50,\n        },\n        stop_loss_percent: {\n          type: \"number\",\n          description: \"Stop-loss percentage for this trade (used for fixed-fraction sizing)\",\n          minimum: 0.1,\n          maximum: 99,\n        },\n        lookback_days: {\n          type: \"integer\",\n          description: \"Days of history to use for win rate calculation (default 30)\",\n          minimum: 1,\n          maximum: 365,\n        },\n      },\n      required: [\"stop_loss_percent\"],\n    },\n    execute: async (params, _context) => {\n      const { mode = \"simulation\", risk_percent = 2, stop_loss_percent, lookback_days = 30 } = params;\n      try {\n        const since = Date.now() - lookback_days * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000;\n        const trades = sdk.db\n          .prepare(\n            `SELECT pnl_percent FROM trade_journal\n             WHERE status = 'closed' AND mode = ? AND timestamp >= ?`\n          )\n          .all(mode, since);\n\n        const balance =\n          mode === \"simulation\"\n            ? getSimBalance(sdk)\n            : parseFloat((await sdk.ton.getBalance())?.balance ?? \"0\");\n\n        let winRate = 0.5; // default if no history\n        let avgWinPct = 5;\n        let avgLossPct = 5;\n\n        if (trades.length >= 5) {\n          const wins = trades.filter((t) => (t.pnl_percent ?? 0) > 0);\n          const lossesArr = trades.filter((t) => (t.pnl_percent ?? 0) < 0);\n          winRate = wins.length / trades.length;\n          avgWinPct = wins.length > 0\n            ? wins.reduce((s, t) => s + (t.pnl_percent ?? 0), 0) / wins.length\n            : 5;\n          avgLossPct = lossesArr.length > 0\n            ? Math.abs(lossesArr.reduce((s, t) => s + (t.pnl_percent ?? 0), 0) / lossesArr.length)\n            : 5;\n        }\n\n        // Kelly Criterion: f* = W/L - (1-W)/W  where W=win_rate, L=loss_rate, b=avg_win/avg_loss\n        const b = avgLossPct > 0 ? avgWinPct / avgLossPct : 1;\n        const kellyFraction = winRate - (1 - winRate) / b;\n        const halfKellyFraction = Math.max(0, kellyFraction / 2); // half-Kelly for safety\n\n        // Fixed-fraction: risk a fixed % of capital, sized so stop-loss = that % of capital\n        const fixedFractionSize = balance * (risk_percent / 100) / (stop_loss_percent / 100);\n\n        return {\n          success: true,\n          data: {\n            mode,\n            balance,\n            trades_analysed: trades.length,\n            win_rate: parseFloat(winRate.toFixed(4)),\n            avg_win_percent: parseFloat(avgWinPct.toFixed(2)),\n            avg_loss_percent: parseFloat(avgLossPct.toFixed(2)),\n            kelly_fraction: parseFloat(kellyFraction.toFixed(4)),\n            half_kelly_fraction: parseFloat(halfKellyFraction.toFixed(4)),\n            kelly_position_size: parseFloat((balance * halfKellyFraction).toFixed(4)),\n            fixed_fraction_position_size: parseFloat(fixedFractionSize.toFixed(4)),\n            risk_percent,\n            stop_loss_percent,\n            recommendation: kellyFraction <= 0\n              ? \"Kelly suggests no position — unfavorable win/loss ratio based on history\"\n              : `Suggested position: ${Math.min(balance * halfKellyFraction, fixedFractionSize).toFixed(2)} TON (lower of Kelly and fixed-fraction)`,\n          },\n        };\n      } catch (err) {\n        sdk.log.error(`ton_trading_get_optimal_position_size failed: ${err.message}`);\n        return { success: false, error: String(err.message).slice(0, 500) };\n      }\n    },\n  },\n\n  // ── P2 Tools: Automation ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n  // ── Tool 22: ton_trading_schedule_trade ────────────────────────────────────\n  {\n    name: \"ton_trading_schedule_trade\",\n    description:\n      \"Store a pending trade to be executed at a future time. The LLM should check scheduled trades on each run and execute any that are due. Returns the scheduled trade ID.\",\n    category: \"action\",\n    parameters: {\n      type: \"object\",\n      properties: {\n        mode: {\n          type: \"string\",\n          description: 'Trading mode: \"real\" or \"simulation\"',\n          enum: [\"real\", \"simulation\"],\n        },\n        from_asset: {\n          type: \"string\",\n          description: 'Asset to sell — \"TON\" or a jetton master address',\n        },\n        to_asset: {\n          type: \"string\",\n          description: 'Asset to buy — \"TON\" or a jetton master address',\n        },\n        amount: {\n          type: \"number\",\n          description: \"Amount of from_asset to trade\",\n        },\n        execute_at_iso: {\n          type: \"string\",\n          description: 'ISO 8601 datetime when to execute the trade (e.g. \"2025-01-01T12:00:00Z\")',\n        },\n        note: {\n          type: \"string\",\n          description: \"Optional note describing the reason for scheduling\",\n        },\n      },\n      required: [\"mode\", \"from_asset\", \"to_asset\", \"amount\", \"execute_at_iso\"],\n    },\n    execute: async (params, _context) => {\n      const { mode, from_asset, to_asset, amount, execute_at_iso, note } = params;\n      try {\n        const executeAt = new Date(execute_at_iso).getTime();\n        if (isNaN(executeAt)) {\n          return { success: false, error: `Invalid execute_at_iso: \"${execute_at_iso}\"` };\n        }\n        if (executeAt <= Date.now()) {\n          return { success: false, error: \"execute_at_iso must be in the future\" };\n        }\n\n        const schedId = sdk.db\n          .prepare(\n            `INSERT INTO scheduled_trades (created_at, execute_at, mode, from_asset, to_asset, amount, note)\n             VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`\n          )\n          .run(Date.now(), executeAt, mode, from_asset, to_asset, amount, note ?? null)\n          .lastInsertRowid;\n\n        sdk.log.info(`Scheduled trade #${schedId}: ${amount} ${from_asset} → ${to_asset} at ${execute_at_iso}`);\n\n        return {\n          success: true,\n          data: {\n            scheduled_id: schedId,\n            mode,\n            from_asset,\n            to_asset,\n            amount,\n            execute_at: execute_at_iso,\n            status: \"pending\",\n          },\n        };\n      } catch (err) {\n        sdk.log.error(`ton_trading_schedule_trade failed: ${err.message}`);\n        return { success: false, error: String(err.message).slice(0, 500) };\n      }\n    },\n  },\n\n  // ── Tool 23: ton_trading_get_scheduled_trades ──────────────────────────────\n  {\n    name: \"ton_trading_get_scheduled_trades\",\n    description:\n      \"List pending scheduled trades. Returns all pending trades, highlighting those that are due now (execute_at <= current time). The LLM should execute due trades using ton_trading_execute_swap or ton_trading_simulate_trade.\",\n    category: \"data-bearing\",\n    parameters: {\n      type: \"object\",\n      properties: {\n        status: {\n          type: \"string\",\n          description: 'Filter by status: \"pending\", \"executed\", \"cancelled\", or \"all\" (default \"pending\")',\n          enum: [\"pending\", \"executed\", \"cancelled\", \"all\"],\n        },\n        limit: {\n          type: \"integer\",\n          description: \"Maximum number of records to return (1–50, default 20)\",\n          minimum: 1,\n          maximum: 50,\n        },\n      },\n    },\n    execute: async (params, _context) => {\n      const status = params.status ?? \"pending\";\n      const limit = params.limit ?? 20;\n      try {\n        const [statusClause, statusParams] = status === \"all\"\n          ? [\"\", []]\n          : [\"WHERE status = ?\", [status]];\n\n        const scheduled = sdk.db\n          .prepare(`SELECT * FROM scheduled_trades ${statusClause} ORDER BY execute_at ASC LIMIT ?`)\n          .all(...statusParams, limit);\n\n        const now = Date.now();\n        const annotated = scheduled.map((s) => ({\n          ...s,\n          is_due: s.execute_at <= now,\n          due_in_ms: Math.max(0, s.execute_at - now),\n        }));\n\n        const dueTrades = annotated.filter((s) => s.is_due && s.status === \"pending\");\n\n        return {\n          success: true,\n          data: {\n            scheduled_trades: annotated,\n            due_now: dueTrades.length,\n            note: dueTrades.length > 0\n              ? `${dueTrades.length} trade(s) are due — execute them using ton_trading_execute_swap or ton_trading_simulate_trade`\n              : null,\n          },\n        };\n      } catch (err) {\n        sdk.log.error(`ton_trading_get_scheduled_trades failed: ${err.message}`);\n        return { success: false, error: String(err.message).slice(0, 500) };\n      }\n    },\n  },\n];\n",

assert.ok(\"defaultSlippage\" in mod.manifest.defaultConfig);\n      assert.ok(\"simulationBalance\" in mod.manifest.defaultConfig);\n    });\n  });\n\n  // ── tools export ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  describe(\"tools export\", () => {\n    it(\"exports tools as a function (SDK pattern)\", () => {\n      assert.equal(typeof mod.tools, \"function\");\n    });\n\n    it(\"tools(sdk) returns an array\", () => {\n      const sdk = makeSdk();\n      const toolList = mod.tools(sdk);\n      assert.ok(Array.isArray(toolList));\n    });\n\n    it(\"exports exactly 22 tools\", () => {\n      const sdk = makeSdk();\n      const toolList = mod.tools(sdk);\n      assert.equal(toolList.length, 22);\n    });\n\n    it(\"all tools have name, description, and execute\", () => {\n      const sdk = makeSdk();\n      const toolList = mod.tools(sdk);\n      for (const tool of toolList) {\n        assert.ok(tool.name, `tool should have name`);\n        assert.ok(tool.description, `tool \"${tool.name}\" should have description`);\n        assert.equal(typeof tool.execute, \"function\", `tool \"${tool.name}\" should have execute function`);\n      }\n    });\n\n    it(\"exports migrate function for database setup\", () => {\n      assert.equal(typeof mod.migrate, \"function\");\n    });\n\n    it(\"migrate creates required tables without error\", () => {\n      const executed = [];\n      const mockDb = { exec: (sql) => executed.push(sql) };\n      assert.doesNotThrow(() => mod.migrate(mockDb));\n      // First exec: CREATE TABLE statements; subsequent execs: ALTER TABLE migrations\n      assert.ok(executed.length >= 1);\n      assert.ok(executed[0].includes(\"trade_journal\"));\n      assert.ok(executed[0].includes(\"sim_balance\"));\n      assert.ok(executed[0].includes(\"stop_loss_rules\"));\n      assert.ok(executed[0].includes(\"scheduled_trades\"));\n    });\n\n    it(\"migrate adds entry_price_usd and exit_price_usd columns to trade_journal\", () => {\n      const executed = [];\n      const mockDb = { exec: (sql) => executed.push(sql) };\n      mod.migrate(mockDb);\n      // The CREATE TABLE should include both P&L price columns\n      assert.ok(executed[0].includes(\"entry_price_usd\"), \"CREATE TABLE should include entry_price_usd\");\n      assert.ok(executed[0].includes(\"exit_price_usd\"), \"CREATE TABLE should include exit_price_usd\");\n      // ALTER TABLE migrations should be present for backward compatibility\n      const allSql = executed.join(\"\\n\");\n      assert.ok(allSql.includes(\"ALTER TABLE trade_journal ADD COLUMN entry_price_usd\"), \"should include ALTER for entry_price_usd\");\n      assert.ok(allSql.includes(\"ALTER TABLE trade_journal ADD COLUMN exit_price_usd\"), \"should include ALTER for exit_price_usd\");\n    });\n\n    it(\"migrate handles existing database that already has the columns\", () => {\n      // When columns already exist, ALTER TABLE throws — migrate should not propagate the error\n      const mockDb = {\n        exec: (sql) => {\n          if (sql.includes(\"ALTER TABLE\")) throw new Error(\"duplicate column name\");\n        },\n      };\n      assert.doesNotThrow(() => mod.migrate(mockDb));\n    });\n  });\n\n  // ── = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_get_market_data\");\n      await tool.execute({ from_asset: \"TON\", to_asset: \"EQCxE6test\", amount: \"1\" }, makeContext());\n      assert.ok(storedKey, \"should set a storage key\");\n      assert.ok(storedKey.includes(\"TON\"), \"storage key should contain from_asset\");\n    });\n\n    it(\"returns success even when DEX quote => {\n    it(\"passes validation for a valid real trade within limits\", async () => {\n      const sdk = makeSdk({\n        pluginConfig: { maxTradePercent: 10, minBalanceTON: 1 },\n      });\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_validate_trade\");\n      // balance = 100.5, 10% = 10.05, trading 5 TON\n      const result = await tool.execute({ mode: \"real\", amount_ton: 5 }, makeContext());\n      assert.equal(result.success, true);\n      assert.equal(result.data.passed, true);\n      assert.equal(result.data.issues.length, 0);\n    });\n\n    it(\"fails when trade exceeds max trade percent\", async () => {\n      const sdk = makeSdk({\n        pluginConfig: { maxTradePercent: 10, minBalanceTON: 1 },\n      });\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_validate_trade\");\n      // balance = 100.5, 10% = 10.05, trading 20 TON (exceeds limit)\n      const result = await tool.execute({ mode: \"real\", amount_ton: 20 }, makeContext());\n      assert.equal(result.success, true);\n      assert.equal(result.data.passed, false);\n      assert.ok(result.data.issues.some((i) => i.type === \"exceeds_max_trade_percent\"));\n    });\n\n    it(\"fails when trade exceeds available balance\", async () => {\n      const sdk = makeSdk({\n        pluginConfig: { maxTradePercent: 100, minBalanceTON: 1 },\n      });\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_validate_trade\");\n      // balance = 100.5, trading 200 TON (exceeds balance)\n      const result = await tool.execute({ mode: \"real\", amount_ton: 200 }, makeContext());\n      assert.equal(result.success, true);\n      assert.equal(result.data.passed, false);\n      assert.ok(result.data.issues.some((i) => i.type === \"exceeds_balance\"));\n    });\n\n    it(\"fails when balance is below minimum\", async () => {\n      const sdk = makeSdk({\n        ton: {\n          getAddress: () => \"EQTestWalletAddress\",\n          getBalance: async () => ({ balance: \"0.5\", balanceNano: \"500000000\" }),\n          getPrice: async () => null,\n          getJettonBalances: async () => [],\n          dex: { quote: async () => null, swap: async () => null },\n        },\n        pluginConfig: { maxTradePercent: 10, minBalanceTON: 1 },\n      });\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_validate_trade\");\n      const result = await tool.execute({ mode: \"real\", amount_ton: 0.1 }, makeContext());\n      assert.equal(result.success, true);\n      assert.equal(result.data.passed, false);\n      assert.ok(result.data.issues.some((i) => i.type === \"insufficient_balance\"));\n    });\n\n    it(\"uses },\n      });\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_validate_trade\");\n      const result = await tool.execute({ mode: \"simulation\", amount_ton: 50 }, ──────────────────────────────────────────────\n  describe(\"ton_trading_simulate_trade\", () => {\n    it(\"records a simulation trade and returns trade_id\", async () => {\n      const sdk =      );\n      assert.equal(result.success, true);\n      assert.equal(result.data.mode, \"simulation\");\n      assert.equal(result.data.trade_id, 7);\n      assert.equal(result.data.status, from_asset: \"TON\", to_asset: \"EQCxE6test\", amount_in: 50, expected_amount_out: 500 },\n        makeContext()\n      );\n      assert.equal(result.success, false);\n      assert.ok(result.error.includes(\"Insufficient simulation balance\"));\n    });\n\n    it(\"returns failure when trade would drop balance below minimum\", async () => {\n      const sdk = makeSdk({\n        dbRows: { simBalance: { balance: 2 } },\n        pluginConfig: { minBalanceTON: 1 },\n      });\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_simulate_trade\");\n      // balance=2, min=1, trading 1.5 → leaves 0.5 which is below min\n      const result = await tool.execute(\n        { from_asset: \"TON\", to_asset: \"EQCxE6test\", amount_in: 1.5, expected_amount_out: 15 },\n        makeContext()\n      );\n      assert.equal(result.success, false);\n      assert.ok(result.error.includes(\"minimum\"));\n    });\n\n    it(\"deducts amount from simulation balance when from_asset is TON\", async () => {\n      let savedBalance = null;\n      const sdk = makeSdk({\n        dbRows: { simBalance: { balance: 100 }, lastInsertRowid: 1 },\n        pluginConfig: { minBalanceTON: 1 },\n        db: {\n          exec: () => {},\n          prepare: (sql) => ({\n            get: () => ({ balance: 100 }),\n            all: () => [],\n            run: (...args) => {\n              if (sql.includes(\"INSERT INTO sim_balance\")) savedBalance = args[1];\n              return { lastInsertRowid: 1 };\n            },\n          }),\n        },\n      });\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_simulate_trade\");\n      await tool.execute(\n        { from_asset: \"TON\", to_asset: \"EQCxE6test\", amount_in: 10, expected_amount_out: 100 },\n        makeContext()\n      );\n      assert.equal(savedBalance, 90, \"simulation balance should be deducted by amount_in\");\n    });\n\n    it(\"required parameters include from_asset, to_asset, amount_in, expected_amount_out\", () => {\n      const sdk = makeSdk();\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_simulate_trade\");\n      assert.ok(tool.parameters?.required?.includes(\"from_asset\"));\n      assert.ok(tool.parameters?.required?.includes(\"to_asset\"));\n      assert.ok(tool.parameters?.required?.includes(\"amount_in\"));\n      assert.ok(tool.parameters?.required?.includes(\"expected_amount_out\"));\n    });\n  });\n\n  // ── ton_trading_execute_swap ────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  describe(\"ton_trading_execute_swap\", () => {\n    it(\"executes a DEX swap and returns trade_id\", async () => {\n      const sdk = makeSdk({\n        dbRows: { lastInsertRowid: 3 },\n      });\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_execute_swap\");\n      const result = await tool.execute(\n        { from_asset: assert.equal(result.data.status, \"open\");\n      assert.equal(result.data.from_asset, \"TON\");\n    });\n\n    it(\"returns failure when wallet is not initialized\", async () => {\n      const sdk = makeSdk({\n        ton: {\n          getAddress: () => null,\n          getBalance: async () => null,\n          getPrice: async () => null,\n          getJettonBalances: async () => [],\n          dex: { quote: async () => null, swap: async () => null },\n        },\n      });\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_execute_swap\");\n      const result = await \"EQTestWalletAddress\",\n          getBalance: async () => null,\n          getPrice: async () => null,\n          getJettonBalances: async () => [],\n          dex: {\n            quote: async () => \"ton_trading_execute_swap\");\n      const result = await tool.execute(\n        { from_asset: \"TON\", to_asset: \"EQCxE6test\", amount: \"2\" },\n        makeContext()\n      );\n      assert.equal(result.success, false);\n      assert.ok(result.error.includes(\"insufficient liquidity\"));\n    });\n\n    it(\"is dm-only scope\", () => {\n      const sdk = makeSdk();\n      const amount\", () => {\n      const sdk = makeSdk();\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_execute_swap\");\n      assert.ok(tool.parameters?.required?.includes(\"from_asset\"));\n      assert.ok(tool.parameters?.required?.includes(\"to_asset\"));\n      assert.ok(tool.parameters?.required?.includes(\"amount\"));\n    });\n  });\n\n  // ── ton_trading_record_trade ────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  describe(\"ton_trading_record_trade\", () => {\n    it(\"closes an open trade and records PnL\", async () => {\n      const openTrade = {\n        id: 1,\n        mode: \"simulation\",\n        from_asset: \"TON\",\n        to_asset: \"EQCxE6test\",\n        amount_in: 10,\n        amount_out: null,\n        status: \"open\",\n      };\n      const sdk = makeSdk({\n        dbRows: { trade: openTrade },\n      });\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_record_trade\");\n      const result = await tool.execute({ trade_id: 1, amount_out: 12 }, makeContext());\n      assert.equal(result.success, true);\n      assert.equal(result.data.amount_in, 10);\n      assert.equal(result.data.amount_out, 12);\n      assert.equal(result.data.pnl, 2);\n      assert.equal(result.data.status, \"closed\");\n      assert.equal(result.data.profit_or_loss, \"profit\");\n    });\n\n    it(\"calculates loss correctly\", async () => {\n      const openTrade = { id: 2, mode: \"real\", amount_in: 10, status: \"open\", to_asset: \"EQCxE6test\" };\n      const sdk = makeSdk({ dbRows: { trade: openTrade } });\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_record_trade\");\n      const result = await tool.execute({ trade_id: 2, amount_out: 8 }, makeContext());\n      assert.equal(result.success, true);\n      assert.equal(result.data.pnl, -2);\n      assert.equal(result.data.profit_or_loss, \"loss\");\n    });\n\n    it(\"returns failure when trade is not found\", async () => {\n      const sdk = makeSdk({ dbRows: { trade: null } });\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_record_trade\");\n      const result = await tool.execute({ trade_id: 999, amount_out: 10 }, makeContext());\n      assert.equal(result.success, false);\n      assert.ok(result.error.includes(\"not found\"));\n    });\n\n    it(\"returns failure when trade is already closed\", async () => {\n      const closedTrade = { id: 3, mode: \"real\", amount_in: 10, status: \"closed\" };\n      const sdk = makeSdk({ dbRows: { trade: closedTrade } });\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_record_trade\");\n      const result = await tool.execute({ trade_id: 3, amount_out: 10 }, makeContext());\n      assert.equal(result.success, false);\n      assert.ok(result.error.includes(\"already closed\"));\n    });\n\n    it(\"required parameters include trade_id and amount_out\", () => {\n      const sdk = makeSdk();\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_record_trade\");\n      assert.ok(tool.parameters?.required?.includes(\"trade_id\"));\n      assert.ok(tool.parameters?.required?.includes(\"amount_out\"));\n    });\n\n    it(\"calculates P&L in USD when currencies differ (USDT→TON trade)\", async () => {\n      // Reproduces issue #80: 50 USDT → 37.6 TON at entry_price_usd=1 (USDT),\n      // exit_price_usd=1.33 (TON price in USD). Real P&L should be ~$0, not -$12.40.\n      const openTrade = {\n        id: 10,\n        mode: \"simulation\",\n        from_asset: \"USDT\",\n        to_asset: \"TON\",\n        amount_in: 50,       // 50 USDT spent\n        entry_price_usd: 1,  // 1 USDT = $1\n        amount_out: null,\n        status: \"open\",\n      };\n      const sdk = makeSdk({ dbRows: { trade: openTrade } });\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_record_trade\");\n      // Closing: received 37.6 TON at $1.33/TON = $50.008 USD value\n      const result = await tool.execute(\n        { trade_id: 10, amount_out: 37.6, exit_price_usd: 1.33 },\n        makeContext()\n      );\n      assert.equal(result.success, true);\n      // USD value out = 37.6 * 1.33 = 50.008; USD value in = 50 * 1 = 50\n      // pnl = 50.008 - 50 = 0.008 (small positive, not -12.40)\n      assert.ok(result.data.pnl > 0, `pnl should be slightly positive (~0.008), got ${result.data.pnl}`);\n      assert.ok(result.data.pnl < 1, `pnl should be near zero, got ${result.data.pnl}`);\n      assert.equal(result.data.profit_or_loss, \"profit\");\n    });\n\n    it(\"calculates P&L correctly for TON→USDT trade using USD prices\", async () => {\n      // Buying USDT with TON: 37.6 TON at $1.33 → 50 USDT\n      const openTrade = {\n        id: 11,\n        mode: \"simulation\",\n        from_asset: \"TON\",\n        to_asset: \"USDT\",\n        amount_in: 37.6,     // 37.6 TON spent\n        entry_price_usd: 1.33, // TON = $1.33\n        amount_out: null,\n        status: \"open\",\n      };\n      const sdk = makeSdk({ dbRows: { trade: openTrade } });\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_record_trade\");\n      // Closing: received 50 USDT at $1/USDT\n      const result = await tool.execute(\n        { trade_id: 11, amount_out: 50, exit_price_usd: 1 },\n        makeContext()\n      );\n      assert.equal(result.success, true);\n      // USD value out = 50 * 1 = 50; USD value in = 37.6 * 1.33 = 50.008\n      // pnl = 50 - 50.008 = -0.008 (tiny loss, not a huge number)\n      assert.ok(result.data.pnl < 0, `pnl should be slightly negative, got ${result.data.pnl}`);\n      assert.ok(result.data.pnl > -1, `pnl should be near zero, got ${result.data.pnl}`);\n      assert.equal(result.data.profit_or_loss, \"loss\");\n    });\n\n    it(\"falls back to raw amount diff when no USD prices provided (backward compat)\", async () => {\n      const openTrade = {\n        id: 12,\n        mode: \"simulation\",\n        from_asset: \"TON\",\n        to_asset: \"TON\",\n        amount_in: 10,\n        amount_out: null,\n        status: \"open\",\n      };\n      const sdk = makeSdk({ dbRows: { trade: openTrade } });\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_record_trade\");\n      const result = await tool.execute({ trade_id: 12, amount_out: 11 }, makeContext());\n ──────────────────────────────────────────────\n  describe(\"ton_trading_simulate_trade\", () => {\n    it(\"records a simulation trade and returns trade_id\", async () => {\n      const sdk = \"ton_trading_simulate_trade\");\n      const result = await tool.execute(\n        { from_asset: \"TON\", to_asset: \"EQCxE6test\", amount_in: 10, expected_amount_out: 100 },\n        makeContext()\n      );\n      assert.equal(result.success, true);\n      assert.equal(result.data.mode, \"simulation\");\n      assert.equal(result.data.trade_id, 7);\n      assert.equal(result.data.status, \"open\");\n    });\n\n    it(\"returns failure when simulation balance is insufficient\", async () => {\n      const sdk = makeSdk({\n        dbRows: { simBalance: { balance: 5 } },\n        pluginConfig: { minBalanceTON: 1 },\n      });\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_simulate_trade\");\n      const result = await tool.execute(\n        { from_asset: \"TON\", to_asset: \"EQCxE6test\", amount_in: 50, expected_amount_out: 500 },\n        makeContext()\n      );\n      assert.equal(result.success, false);\n      assert.ok(result.error.includes(\"Insufficient simulation balance\"));\n    });\n\n    it(\"returns failure when trade would drop balance below minimum\", async () => {\n      const sdk = makeSdk({\n        dbRows: { simBalance: { balance: 2 } },\n        pluginConfig: { minBalanceTON: 1 },\n      });\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_simulate_trade\");\n      // balance=2, min=1, trading 1.5 → leaves 0.5 which is below min\n      const result = await tool.execute(\n        { from_asset: \"TON\", to_asset: \"EQCxE6test\", amount_in: 1.5, expected_amount_out: 15 },\n        makeContext()\n      );\n      assert.equal(result.success, false);\n      assert.ok(result.error.includes(\"minimum\"));\n    });\n\n    it(\"deducts amount from simulation balance when from_asset is TON\", async () => {\n      let savedBalance = null;\n      const sdk = makeSdk({\n        dbRows: { simBalance: { balance: 100 }, lastInsertRowid: 1 },\n        pluginConfig: { minBalanceTON: 1 },\n        db: {\n          exec: () => {},\n          prepare: (sql) => ({\n            get: () => ({ balance: 100 }),\n            all: () => [],\n            run: (...args) => {\n              if (sql.includes(\"INSERT INTO sim_balance\")) savedBalance = args[1];\n              return { lastInsertRowid: 1 };\n            },\n          }),\n        },\n      });\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_simulate_trade\");\n      await tool.execute(\n        { from_asset: \"TON\", to_asset: \"EQCxE6test\", amount_in: 10, expected_amount_out: 100 },\n        makeContext()\n      );\n      assert.equal(savedBalance, 90, \"simulation balance should be deducted by amount_in\");\n    });\n\n    it(\"required parameters include from_asset, to_asset, amount_in, expected_amount_out\", () => {\n      const sdk = makeSdk();\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_simulate_trade\");\n      assert.ok(tool.parameters?.required?.includes(\"from_asset\"));\n      assert.ok(tool.parameters?.required?.includes(\"to_asset\"));\n      assert.ok(tool.parameters?.required?.includes(\"amount_in\"));\n      assert.ok(tool.parameters?.required?.includes(\"expected_amount_out\"));\n    });\n  });\n\n  // ── ton_trading_execute_swap ────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  describe(\"ton_trading_execute_swap\", () => {\n    it(\"executes a DEX swap and returns trade_id\", async () => {\n      const sdk = makeSdk({\n        dbRows: { lastInsertRowid: 3 },\n      });\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_execute_swap\");\n      const result = await tool.execute(\n        { from_asset: assert.equal(result.data.status, \"open\");\n      assert.equal(result.data.from_asset, \"TON\");\n    });\n\n    it(\"returns failure when wallet is not initialized\", async () => {\n      const sdk = makeSdk({\n        ton: {\n          getAddress: () => null,\n          getBalance: async () => null,\n          getPrice: async () => null,\n          getJettonBalances: async () => [],\n          dex: { quote: async () => null, swap: async () => null },\n        },\n      });\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_execute_swap\");\n      const result = await tool.execute(\n        { from_asset: \"TON\", to_asset: \"EQCxE6test\", amount: \"2\" },\n        makeContext()\n      );\n      assert.equal(result.success, false);\n      assert.equal(result.error, \"ton_trading_execute_swap\");\n      const result = await tool.execute(\n        { from_asset: \"TON\", to_asset: \"EQCxE6test\", amount: \"2\" },\n        makeContext()\n      );\n      assert.equal(result.success, false);\n      assert.ok(result.error.includes(\"insufficient liquidity\"));\n    });\n\n    it(\"is dm-only scope\", () => {\n      const sdk = makeSdk();\n      const assert.ok(tool.parameters?.required?.includes(\"amount\"));\n    });\n  });\n\n  // ── ton_trading_record_trade ────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  describe(\"ton_trading_record_trade\", () => {\n    it(\"closes an open trade and records PnL\", async () => {\n      const openTrade = {\n        id: 1,\n        mode: \"simulation\",\n        from_asset: \"TON\",\n        to_asset: \"EQCxE6test\",\n        amount_in: 10,\n        amount_out: null,\n        status: \"open\",\n      };\n      const sdk = makeSdk({\n        dbRows: { trade: openTrade },\n      });\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_record_trade\");\n      const result = await tool.execute({ trade_id: 1, amount_out: 12 }, makeContext());\n      assert.equal(result.success, true);\n      assert.equal(result.data.amount_in, 10);\n      assert.equal(result.data.amount_out, 12);\n      assert.equal(result.data.pnl, 2);\n      assert.equal(result.data.status, \"closed\");\n      assert.equal(result.data.profit_or_loss, \"profit\");\n    });\n\n    it(\"calculates loss correctly\", async () => {\n      const openTrade = { id: 2, mode: \"real\", amount_in: 10, status: \"open\", to_asset: \"EQCxE6test\" };\n      const sdk = makeSdk({ dbRows: { trade: openTrade } });\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_record_trade\");\n      const result = await tool.execute({ trade_id: 2, amount_out: 8 }, makeContext());\n      assert.equal(result.success, true);\n      assert.equal(result.data.pnl, -2);\n      assert.equal(result.data.profit_or_loss, \"loss\");\n    });\n\n    it(\"returns failure when trade is not found\", async () => {\n      const sdk = makeSdk({ dbRows: { trade: null } });\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_record_trade\");\n      const result = await tool.execute({ trade_id: 999, amount_out: 10 }, makeContext());\n      assert.equal(result.success, false);\n      assert.ok(result.error.includes(\"not found\"));\n    });\n\n    it(\"returns failure when trade is already closed\", async () => {\n      const closedTrade = { id: 3, mode: \"real\", amount_in: 10, status: \"closed\" };\n      const sdk = makeSdk({ dbRows: { trade: closedTrade } });\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_record_trade\");\n      const result = await tool.execute({ trade_id: 3, amount_out: 10 }, makeContext());\n      assert.equal(result.success, false);\n      assert.ok(result.error.includes(\"already closed\"));\n    });\n\n    it(\"required parameters include trade_id and amount_out\", () => {\n      const sdk = makeSdk();\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_record_trade\");\n      assert.ok(tool.parameters?.required?.includes(\"trade_id\"));\n      assert.ok(tool.parameters?.required?.includes(\"amount_out\"));\n    });\n\n    it(\"calculates P&L in USD when currencies differ (USDT→TON trade)\", async () => {\n      // Reproduces issue #80: 50 USDT → 37.6 TON at entry_price_usd=1 (USDT),\n      // exit_price_usd=1.33 (TON price in USD). Real P&L should be ~$0, not -$12.40.\n      const openTrade = {\n        id: 10,\n        mode: \"simulation\",\n        from_asset: \"USDT\",\n        to_asset: \"TON\",\n        amount_in: 50,       // 50 USDT spent\n        entry_price_usd: 1,  // 1 USDT = $1\n        amount_out: null,\n        status: \"open\",\n      };\n      const sdk = makeSdk({ dbRows: { trade: openTrade } });\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_record_trade\");\n      // Closing: received 37.6 TON at $1.33/TON = $50.008 USD value\n      const result = await tool.execute(\n        { trade_id: 10, amount_out: 37.6, exit_price_usd: 1.33 },\n        makeContext()\n      );\n      assert.equal(result.success, true);\n      // USD value out = 37.6 * 1.33 = 50.008; USD value in = 50 * 1 = 50\n      // pnl = 50.008 - 50 = 0.008 (small positive, not -12.40)\n      assert.ok(result.data.pnl > 0, `pnl should be slightly positive (~0.008), got ${result.data.pnl}`);\n      assert.ok(result.data.pnl < 1, `pnl should be near zero, got ${result.data.pnl}`);\n      assert.equal(result.data.profit_or_loss, \"profit\");\n    });\n\n    it(\"calculates P&L correctly for TON→USDT trade using USD prices\", async () => {\n      // Buying USDT with TON: 37.6 TON at $1.33 → 50 USDT\n      const openTrade = {\n        id: 11,\n        mode: \"simulation\",\n        from_asset: \"TON\",\n        to_asset: \"USDT\",\n        amount_in: 37.6,     // 37.6 TON spent\n        entry_price_usd: 1.33, // TON = $1.33\n        amount_out: null,\n        status: \"open\",\n      };\n      const sdk = makeSdk({ dbRows: { trade: openTrade } });\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_record_trade\");\n      // Closing: received 50 USDT at $1/USDT\n      const result = await tool.execute(\n        { trade_id: 11, amount_out: 50, exit_price_usd: 1 },\n        makeContext()\n      );\n      assert.equal(result.success, true);\n      // USD value out = 50 * 1 = 50; USD value in = 37.6 * 1.33 = 50.008\n      // pnl = 50 - 50.008 = -0.008 (tiny loss, not a huge number)\n      assert.ok(result.data.pnl < 0, `pnl should be slightly negative, got ${result.data.pnl}`);\n      assert.ok(result.data.pnl > -1, `pnl should be near zero, got ${result.data.pnl}`);\n      assert.equal(result.data.profit_or_loss, \"loss\");\n    });\n\n    it(\"falls back to raw amount diff when no USD prices provided (backward compat)\", async () => {\n      const openTrade = {\n        id: 12,\n        mode: \"simulation\",\n        from_asset: \"TON\",\n        to_asset: \"TON\",\n        amount_in: 10,\n        amount_out: null,\n        status: \"open\",\n      };\n      const sdk = makeSdk({ dbRows: { trade: openTrade } });\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_record_trade\");\n      const result = await tool.execute({ trade_id: 12, amount_out: 11 }, makeContext());\n      assert.equal(result.success, true);\n      assert.equal(result.data.pnl, 1);\n      assert.equal(result.data.profit_or_loss, \"profit\");\n    });\n\n    it(\"ton_trading_simulate_trade accepts and stores entry_price_usd\", async () => {\n      const sdk = makeSdk({ dbRows: { simBalance: { balance: 1000 } } });\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_simulate_trade\");\n      const result = await tool.execute(\n        { from_asset: \"USDT\", to_asset: \"TON\", amount_in: 50, expected_amount_out: 37.6, entry_price_usd: 1 },\n        makeContext()\n      );\n      assert.equal(result.success, true);\n      assert.equal(result.data.entry_price_usd, 1);\n    });\n\n    it(\"ton_trading_record_trade saves exit_price_usd to database\", async () => {\n      const openTrade = {\n        id: 15,\n        mode: \"simulation\",\n        from_asset: \"USDT\",\n        to_asset: \"TON\",\n        amount_in: 50,\n        entry_price_usd: 1,\n        amount_out: null,\n        status: \"open\",\n      };\n      let savedExitPriceUsd = null;\n      const sdk = makeSdk({\n        dbRows: { trade: openTrade },\n        db: {\n          exec: () => {},\n          prepare: (sql) => ({\n            get: () => openTrade,\n            all: () => [],\n            run: (...args) => {\n              if (sql.includes(\"UPDATE trade_journal\")) {\n                savedExitPriceUsd = args[1];\n              }\n              return { lastInsertRowid: 15 };\n            },\n          }),\n        },\n      });\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_record_trade\");\n      const result = await tool.execute(\n        { trade_id: 15, amount_out: 37.6, exit_price_usd: 1.22 },\n        makeContext()\n      );\n      assert.equal(result.success, true);\n      assert.equal(savedExitPriceUsd, 1.22, \"exit_price_usd should be saved to database\");\n    });\n  });\n\n  // ── [2026-03-30T11:37:54.767Z] [INFO]  parameter, not interpolated\", async () => {\n      const capturedSqls = [];\n      const capturedParams = [];\n      const sdk = makeSdk({\n        db: {\n          exec: () => {},\n          prepare: (sql) => {\n            capturedSqls.push(sql);\n            return {\n              get: () => null,\n              all: (...params) => { capturedParams.push(params); return []; },\n              run: () => ({ lastInsertRowid: 1 }),\n            };\n          },\n        },\n      });\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_calculate_risk_metrics\");\n      await tool.execute({ mode: \"real\", lookback_days: 7, confidence_level: 0.95 }, {});\n      const riskSql = capturedSqls.find((s) => s.includes(\"trade_journal\"));\n      assert.ok(riskSql, \"trade_journal query should be captured\");\n      // SQL must not contain the literal mode value — it must use a placeholder\n      assert.ok(!riskSql.includes(\"'real'\"), \"mode value must not be string-interpolated into SQL\");\n      assert.ok(riskSql.includes(\"?\"), \"SQL must use parameterized placeholder\");\n      // The mode value must be passed as a bound parameter\n      const riskParams = capturedParams.find((p) => p.some((v) => v === \"real\"));\n      assert.ok(riskParams, \"mode value 'real' must be passed as a bound parameter\");\n    });\n\n    it(\"ton_trading_calculate_risk_metrics: SQL injection payload is not interpolated\", async () => {\n      const capturedSqls = [];\n      const sdk = makeSdk({\n        db: {\n          exec: () => {},\n          prepare: (sql) => {\n            capturedSqls.push(sql);\n            return {\n              get: () => null,\n              all: () => [],\n              run: () => ({ lastInsertRowid: 1 }),\n            };\n          },\n        },\n      });\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_calculate_risk_metrics\");\n      const injectionPayload = \"real' OR '1'='1\";\n      await tool.execute({ mode: injectionPayload, lookback_days: 7, confidence_level: 0.95 }, {});\n      const riskSql = capturedSqls.find((s) => s.includes(\"trade_journal\"));\n      assert.ok(riskSql, \"trade_journal query should be captured\");\n      assert.ok(!riskSql.includes(injectionPayload), \"SQL injection payload must not appear in query string\");\n    });\n\n    it(\"ton_trading_get_scheduled_trades: status value is passed as parameter, not interpolated\", async () => {\n      const capturedSqls = [];\n      const capturedParams = [];\n      const sdk = makeSdk({\n        db: {\n          exec: () => {},\n          prepare: (sql) => {\n            capturedSqls.push(sql);\n            return {\n              get: () => null,\n              all: (...params) => { capturedParams.push(params); return []; },\n              run: () => ({ lastInsertRowid: 1 }),\n            };\n          },\n        },\n      });\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_get_scheduled_trades\");\n      await tool.execute({ status: \"pending\", limit: 10 }, {});\n      const schedSql = capturedSqls.find((s) => s.includes(\"scheduled_trades\"));\n      assert.ok(schedSql, \"scheduled_trades query should be captured\");\n      assert.ok(!schedSql.includes(\"'pending'\"), \"status value must not be string-interpolated into SQL\");\n      assert.ok(schedSql.includes(\"?\"), \"SQL must use parameterized placeholder\");\n      const schedParams = capturedParams.find((p) => p.some((v) => v === \"pending\"));\n      assert.ok(schedParams, \"status value 'pending' must be passed as a bound parameter\");\n    });\n\n    it(\"ton_trading_get_scheduled_trades: SQL injection payload is not interpolated\", async () => {\n      const capturedSqls = [];\n      const sdk = makeSdk({\n        db: {\n          exec: () => {},\n          prepare: (sql) => {\n            capturedSqls.push(sql);\n            return {\n              get: () => null,\n              all: () => [],\n              run: () => ({ lastInsertRowid: 1 }),\n            };\n          },\n        },\n      });\n      const tool = mod.tools(sdk).find((t) => t.name === \"ton_trading_get_scheduled_trades\");\n      const injectionPayload = \"pending' OR '1'='1\";\n      await tool.execute({ status: injectionPayload, limit: 10 }, {});\n      const schedSql = capturedSqls.find((s) => s.includes(\"scheduled_trades\"));\n      assert.ok(schedSql, \"scheduled_trades query should be captured\");\n      assert.ok(!schedSql.includes(injectionPayload), \"SQL injection payload must not appear in query string\");\n    });\n  });\n});\n",
